data.title: Private Bancorp of America GAAP EPS of $2.07, revenue of $34.5M
data.description:
• Private Bancorp of America press release (PBAM): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.07.
  • Revenue of $34.5M.
  • Loans held-for-investment (HFI) totaled $2.14 billion as of March 31, 2026, an increase of $14.8 million or 0.7% from December 31, 2025.
  • Investment securities available-for-sale (AFS) were $220.9 million as of March 31, 2026, an increase of $3.1 million or 1.41% since December 31, 2025, and an increase of $64.6 million or 41.3% from March 31, 2025, primarily as a result of new securities purchased.
  • Net interest margin was 5.21% for the first quarter of 2026, compared to 4.84% in the prior quarter and 4.61% in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Provision for credit losses for the first quarter of 2026 was $2.0 million, compared to $2.6 million for the prior quarter and $299 thousand for the first quarter of 2025. The allowance for loan losses was 1.41% of loans HFI as of March 31, 2026 compared to 1.38% at December 31, 2025.
  • As of March 31, 2026, criticized loans totaled $68.2 million, or 3.19% of total loans, down from $73.2 million, or 3.44% of total loans at December 31, 2025.
  • Tangible book value per share(1) was $47.38 as of March 31, 2026, an increase of $1.63 since December 31, 2025 primarily as a result of strong earnings.
  •

data.title: Blue Owl executives revise share-backed personal loan terms amid falling stock prices - report
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital (OWL) is revising the terms of personal loans that the alternative asset manager's co-CEOs had taken against their shares amid falling stock prices, people familiar with the matter told the Wall Street Journal.

OWL has lost ~20% of its value since the private credit manager announced curbing redemptions at Blue Owl Capital Corp II and a $1.4B sale of credit assets across three funds, including $600M from the retail fund—representing 30% of its total assets.

Borrowings by Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz are said to have raised concerns in the investor community. OWL's declining stock price could open them up to margin calls.

Ostrover and Lipschultz had pledged over 130M shares, representing roughly two-thirds of their respective stakes, for an undisclosed amount of loans, the report said, citing a February filing.

The co-CEOs have removed the shares as collateral for their loans, the people reportedly said.

An updated public filing could come as soon as today, the report published on Friday, April 17, said.

data.title: Parke Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.99, revenue of $22.98M
data.description:
• Parke Bancorp press release (PKBK): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.99.
  • Revenue of $22.98M.
  • The Company recorded a provision for credit losses of $0.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to a provision for credit losses of $0.6 million for the same period in 2025.
  • Non-interest income increased slightly by $0.03 million, or 3.9%, to $0.85 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to $0.82 million for the same period in 2025.
  • Non-interest expense increased $0.7 million, or 10.4%, to $7.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to $6.5 million for the same period in 2025.

data.title: Chemung Financial GAAP EPS of $1.91, revenue of $29.9M
data.description:
• Chemung Financial press release (CHMG): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.91.
  • Revenue of $29.9M.
  • Quarterly adjusted efficiency ratio of 58.27% was also the lowest in the Corporation's history, an improvement of 1.85% compared to the fourth quarter of 2025 and 7.37% compared to the first quarter of 2025. 1
  • Annualized loan growth totaled 7.5%, including period-end growth of nearly 15% in the Western New York Canal Bank division. Loan growth in the Capital Bank division exceeded 7%, annualized.
  • In March, the Corporation opened a representative office in downtown Buffalo, New York to support the growing operations of its Canal Bank division.
  • Dividends declared during the first quarter of 2026 were $0.34 per share.

data.title: Meta to start companywide layoffs May 20 -- Reuters
data.description:
Meta (META) is reportedly planning its first wave of layoffs on May 20, according to sources cited by Reuters.

The job cuts will involve roughly 8,000 individuals, representing 10% of the company’s global staff, in what is considered an “initial round” of job cuts, with further cuts expected in the second half of this year.

This round of layoffs will be the most significant for the company since late 2022 and early 2023, when Meta (META) eliminated 21K jobs in what it called a “year of efficiency” after growth assumptions post-COVID proved unsustainable. 

While it’s not yet confirmed what prompted the job cuts, the company is heavily invested in AI technology, mirroring a similar pattern among other tech companies that ultimately found AI required fewer staff.

The news gave a modest boost to the company’s stock into the close.

Meta has not yet responded to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment.

data.title: Merck gains after six-day losing streak
data.description:
Merck (NYSE: MRK) rose after six straight sessions of losses, with shares closing up 3.07% at $119.01 on Friday.

Over the prior six sessions, the stock had fallen 6.27%, underperforming a 3.81% gain in the S&P 500 Index. However, the shares are up 11.05% in 2026, compared with a 2.86% rise in the broader index.

According to Seeking Alpha analyst Edmund Ingham, Merck’s agreement to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals for $53 per share, valuing the deal at about $6.7 billion, had been viewed as a move to strengthen its oncology pipeline, with the analyst maintaining a Buy rating on the stock.

 He had noted that early-stage data for TERN-701 had shown strong response rates, outperforming existing therapies such as Novartis’ Scemblix. The deal had been considered part of Merck’s strategy to offset the upcoming Keytruda patent expiry, with TERN-701 positioned as a potential growth driver, while execution and regulatory risks had remained.

Separately, in a recent update, the European Commission had approved Merck’s Keytruda-based combination therapy with chemotherapy (paclitaxel) for certain adults with ovarian cancer, expanding its use as a late-line treatment option in platinum-resistant disease. The decision had followed earlier endorsement from the European Medicines Agency, which had supported the marketing application for the indication last month.

Meanwhile, according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, MRK held a Hold rating with a score of 3.26 out of 5, with the stock rated A+ for profitability, D for growth, and B+ for momentum.

According to Wall Street analysts, 19 rated the stock a Buy or above, 10 rated it a Hold, while none rated it a Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts also remained broadly positive on the stock, maintaining a Buy call for MRK.

data.title: British American Tobacco shares rose after six-session losing streak
data.description:
British American Tobacco (BTI) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock was up 0.9% at $56.62 on Friday.

The tobbacco and nicotine company fell 4.6% in the preceding six sessions while the company is down 3% over the past one month.

Recently, BTI introduced Dragos Constantinescu as chief financial officer and executive director with effect from September 1, 2026.

"BTI's overall performance in the past year, its outlook for 2026 and beyond, the market multiples and dividends, all work in its favour too," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, BTI has a Hold rating with a score of 3.2 out of 5. The company received an A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D+ in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, four analysts gave BTI a Strong Buy rating. One analyst has given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Strong Sell.

 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

data.title: Nvidia named as participant in Cursor's current $2B funding round: report
data.description:
Cursor, a popular AI-powered code editor, is currently involved in a $2B funding round that would elevate its valuation to more than $50B, according to a report by Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the deal.

The tech-focused venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz was named as a co-leader in the round, the report said. Thrive Capital and Nvidia (NVDA) are also expected to participate.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has regularly discussed his respect for Cursor and indicated that all the developers at his company use it.

AI-assisted coding has emerged as one of the most popular uses of the rising technology. Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude Code and OpenAI's (OPENAI) Codex have both gained traction among individual developers and enterprises. 

Cursor's annualized revenue surpassed $2B in February, according to Fortune. It is estimated that 67% of Fortune 500 companies are utilizing Cursor. Its platform generates 150M lines of enterprise code every day. The startup was founded in 2023 by four recent MIT graduates, including Michael Truell, who serves as CEO.

data.title: Fifth Third outlines $8.7B-$8.8B 2026 NII outlook as Comerica integration targets $850M run-rate savings by Q4
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "Today, we reported earnings per share of $0.15 or $0.83 excluding certain items... Results reflect the February 1 closing of the Comerica acquisition." (Chairman, CEO & President Timothy Spence)
  • "We remain confident that we will deliver $360 million of net cost savings this year and reach an $850 million annual run rate by the fourth quarter." (CEO Spence)
  • "Commercial Payments, Newline continued to scale with revenue up 30% and deposits up $2.7 billion year-over-year." (CEO Spence)
  • "We are closely evaluating the direct impacts of the war in Iran on energy and other commodities as well as the implications for prices, interest rates and customer activity." (CEO Spence)
  • "Results exceeded our March expectations, driven by stronger NII, disciplined expense management and integration execution on plan." (Executive VP & CFO Bryan Preston)

Outlook

  • "We are updating our full year NII outlook to a range between $8.7 billion and $8.8 billion." (CFO Preston)
  • "Full year noninterest income is expected to be between $4.0 billion and $4.2 billion." (CFO Preston)
  • "Full year noninterest expense is expected to be $7.2 billion to $7.3 billion... and $360 million of net expense synergies in 2026." (CFO Preston)
  • "We expect to resume regular quarterly share repurchases in the second half of 2026, with the amount and timing dependent on the balance sheet growth and the timing of the remaining merger-related charges." (CFO Preston)
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s rate-curve assumption, management said the current outlook "reflects the forward curve at the end of March, which assumes no rate cuts or hikes in 2026." (CFO Preston)

Financial Results

  • "Revenue was $2.9 billion... and adjusted net income was $734 million." (CEO Spence)
  • "Net interest income was $1.94 billion for the quarter... Net interest margin expanded 17 basis points to 330 basis points." (CFO Preston)
  • "Adjusted noninterest income... was $921 million." (CFO Preston)
  • "Page 5 of our release details... primarily $635 million in merger-related expenses." (CFO Preston)
  • "The net charge-off ratio was 37 basis points for the quarter... The NPA ratio was 57 basis points compared to 65 basis points last quarter." (CFO Preston)

Q&A

  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC: asked what has improved in integration execution and where "snags" could emerge; CEO Spence said "there really haven't been big surprises" and highlighted Texas promotion results including "We mailed 700,000 households" and a follow-on "to 6 million people," adding "we actually expect that campaign alone to generate $1 billion in deposits" while calling tech conversion "the single largest point of risk." (CEO Spence)
  • Robert Siefers, Piper Sandler & Co.: asked about margin drivers and competition; CFO Preston said loan and deposit competition is "competitive but not irrational" and discussed a NIM trajectory "approaching to exiting the year closer to 340". (CFO Preston)
  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC Capital Markets: asked about utilization and delinquency; CFO Preston cited utilization pickup and CEO Spence noted Fifth Third did not rely on private-capital-driven growth, adding "we have less than $100 million of funded exposure to data centers"; on delinquency, CEO Spence said "the majority of the increase... was 2 credits and the payments got made on April 1." (CEO Spence; CFO Preston)
  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities: pressed on deposit strategy and branch relevance; CEO Spence tied Southeast "3%" overall household growth and "8%" in the Southeast to primary checking conversion, and said branches act as "attributes" that lift direct-marketing response rates. (CEO Spence)
  • Peter Winter, D.A. Davidson: asked about revenue-synergy upside and rate-risk repositioning; CFO Preston said revenue synergy would be "upside" and described "$30 billion to $40 billion of kind of notional exposure" available to adjust along the curve. (CFO Preston)

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts sentiment was slightly negative to neutral, with repeated execution-risk framing around the merger and conversion, including "where are you seeing some of the snags?" (Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC).
  • Management sentiment was slightly positive and confident on integration and balance sheet discipline, including "the integration is on track" and "we remain confident." (CFO Preston; CEO Spence).
  • Versus the prior quarter, the tone shifted from pre-close planning to post-close execution detail, with more direct emphasis on conversion risk and near-term campaign performance in Texas. (CEO Spence)

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management moved from "We expect to close on February 1" to confirming close and stating results "reflect the February 1 closing of the Comerica acquisition." (CEO Spence)
  • Guidance language changed on rates: Q4 used a curve that "assumed 25 basis point rate cuts" while Q1 said the curve "assumes no rate cuts or hikes in 2026," alongside an updated full-year NII range of $8.7 billion to $8.8 billion. (CFO Preston)
  • The integration timeline emphasis remained centered on Labor Day systems conversion and achieving the "$850 million annual run rate" savings by Q4, but Q1 added early synergy proof points (client pitches, pipeline activity) rather than solely describing targets. (CEO Spence)

Risks and Concerns

  • "The tech conversion... is the single largest point of risk" because service disruptions could prevent customers from accessing accounts. (CEO Spence)
  • Management flagged macro uncertainty, saying it is evaluating "the direct impacts of the war in Iran on energy and other commodities" and potential implications for "prices, interest rates and customer activity." (CEO Spence)
  • On portfolio posture, management described deliberate limits in select areas, including "we have chosen not to participate meaningfully in lending to private credit vehicles" and "our exposure is intentionally limited" in software and data center lending. (CFO Preston)

Final Takeaway

Management framed Q1 as the first quarter of operating with Comerica closed, highlighting integration milestones toward a Labor Day conversion and reiterating confidence in delivering $360 million of net cost savings in 2026 and an $850 million annualized run-rate by Q4, while updating full-year 2026 financial expectations (including $8.7 billion to $8.8 billion in NII) under a forward curve that assumes no rate moves in 2026 and repeatedly pointing to tech conversion execution and geopolitical-driven commodity volatility as key swing factors.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Most shorted mid cap stocks as of mid-April
data.description:
Short sellers are concentrating their bets heavily in the mid-cap space as of mid-April. The following list ranks the 10 most heavily shorted mid-cap stocks by short interest as a percentage of float.

These U.S.-listed primary tickers have market capitalizations ranging from approximately $2.4B to $8.6B, spanning sectors including application software, communications equipment, health care services, and biotechnology.

The list is topped by SoundHound AI, Inc. (SOUN), with a short interest of 35.75% of float. Ondas Inc. (ONDS) and CleanSpark, Inc. (CLSK) follow closely behind, each with more than a third of their float sold short.

Hims & Hers Health, Inc. (HIMS) and MARA Holdings, Inc. (MARA) round out the top five most heavily shorted names, with short interest percentages of 32.50% and 29.76%, respectively. Further down the list, Applied Digital Corporation (APLD) and Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR) also carry significant short positions, with short interest above 26%.

Here is the list:

  1. SoundHound AI, Inc. (SOUN), Short interest: 35.75%

  2. Ondas Inc. (ONDS), Short interest: 34.11%

  3. CleanSpark, Inc. (CLSK), Short interest: 33.68%

  4. Hims & Hers Health, Inc. (HIMS), Short interest: 32.50%

  5. MARA Holdings, Inc. (MARA), Short interest: 29.76%

  6. Upstart Holdings, Inc. (UPST), Short interest: 29.14%

  7. Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (EOSE), Short interest: 29.03%

  8. Tango Therapeutics, Inc. (TNGX), Short interest: 28.29%

  9. Applied Digital Corporation (APLD), Short interest: 27.08%

  10. Sphere Entertainment Co. (SPHR), Short interest: 26.15%

Mid-Cap ETFs: (IJH), (VO), (IWR), (MDY), (VOE), (VOT), (SCHM), (IJK), and (IJJ)
